notes: Evidence-based supplement database; 129 studies, 107 RCTs, 1.27M participants; updated Feb 2026; covers mortality, immune, bone, mood
BJPsychiatry 2013 meta-analysis (14 studies, 31k+ participants) — association between low vitamin D and depression; supports mood benefit claim; does not establish causation
Reddit post — not a primary source; underlying C. elegans lifespan study exists but must be cited directly
2023 meta-analysis of 80 RCTs, 163k participants — vitamin D supplementation associated with reduced all-cause mortality (OR 0.95); best current mortality evidence
BMJ 2019 systematic review and meta-analysis of 50 RCTs, 74,655 participants — vitamin D supplementation associated with 7% lower all-cause mortality; strong supporting evidence

VITAL trial 2025 AJCN — 1054 participants, 4yr RCT; 2000 IU/day vitamin D3 reduced telomere attrition by 140bp over 4 years (~3yr equivalent biological aging preserved); omega-3 had no significant effect on telomere length in this trial

Vitamin D promotes protein homeostasis and longevity via SKN-1, IRE-1, and XBP-1 stress response pathways in C. elegans; directly sources the animal model lifespan extension mechanism referenced in the resource body
